subject: Project Management
title: Scrum Gone Wrong
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Scrum Gone Wrong.

hook.text: Half-hearted Scrum is worse than no Scrum. Teams call it 'Scrum-but' — Scrum, but we skip the retro.

buildup.text: Scrum-but: we do sprints, but the boss adds work mid-sprint. We have a PO, but they never show up.

discovery.text: The most common anti-pattern: treating Scrum as a set of meetings, not a change in how teams work.

twist.text: Bad Scrum gives Scrum a bad name. Teams blame the framework when the problem is the implementation.

climax.text: Scrum demands commitment from the whole organization — half-measures produce half-results.

punchline.text: Scrum-but is just 'but' with extra meetings.

quiz.question: What is 'Scrum-but'?
options: Doing Scrum but skipping key practices
A variant of Scrum for small teams
